name: Chicken Burgers with Mustard Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix the Dijon and Coleman's mustard.
2. Heat 1 tablespoon of oil in a saucepan.
3. Add chopped onion and celery to the pan; sauté until tender.
4. Add white wine vinegar and reduce by half.
5. Stir in the mustard mixture, brown stock, black pepper, salt, and Worcestershire sauce into the saucepan.
6. Cook for 30 minutes.
7. Strain into a sauceboat.
1. To make burgers, grind chicken and veal together. Stir in parsley, 1 1/2 teaspoons of salt, white pepper, Worcestershire sauce, and hot pepper sauce.
8. Soak bread slices in milk; add to the meat mixture along with egg, mixing well.
9. Heat 2 tablespoons of oil in a skillet.
10. Add sliced onion to the pan; cook until barely tender.
11. Add mushrooms, bay leaf, and thyme to the pan. Sauté until liquid has evaporated.
12. Remove the bay leaf from the mixture.
13. Add mushroom mixture to meat mixture; combine by stirring 2 to 4 minutes or process in a food processor until almost smooth.
14. Form into patties and coat with bread crumbs.
15. Melt butter in a large skillet.
16. Cook patties until browned on both sides, then remove from the skillet.
17. When ready to serve, place browned patties in a baking pan and bake at 375°F for 10 minutes to crisp. Serve with mustard sauce.
